behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is the segmentation algorithm itself (single boundary), not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; a pure input string reproduces the failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text only asserts segmentation correctness, so higher-level wiring is not required to expose this bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmenter with a multi-line shell snippet containing assignments and a command; assert returned segments are split per statement/newline boundaries and not a single combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when segmentation output is consumed by trigger classification (module boundary), matching the incident. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/network required if hook input is simulated; System rejected because behavior text is about classification of command text, not real `gh` execution. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse classification pipeline (segment + trigger detector) on a multi-line block (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`) and assert final classification is PR-create trigger, not `not_a_trigger`.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on segmented-command interpretation plus trigger predicate specificity across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real GitHub/API/subprocess required; System rejected because behavior text is command-type discrimination only. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the same multiline pattern but with final command `gh issue create ...` through the same pipeline; assert classification is non-PR trigger (e.g., `not_a_trigger` for PR loop purposes).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug boundary is detection output + identifier extraction + state persistence wiring. Unit tests of extractor alone can miss persistence contract failures. REAL-INFRA: local temp filesystem is sufficient; no real HTTP/subprocess required, so System rejected by behavior text (no requirement to invoke live `gh`). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Given a simulated PostToolUse event for PR creation (including command/output metadata), execute hook flow and assert persisted state file contains reconstructed PR identifier in expected field/value format.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module handoff (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local filesystem + in-process hook invocation; no real OS-level watcher/network/subprocess requirement, so System rejected. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: not an agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp workspace, run PostToolUse handling for a PR-create trigger to write state, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against that state and assert enforcement decision reflects active review gate.
